meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy: A Student Checklist for AI Builders
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' block by building SaaS pricing models. Use this expert checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first app.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for the 'perfect' idea. Instead, master the mechanics of SaaS pricing models. By engineering a subscription engine, usage-based billing, or tier-based access control, you gain the technical architecture skills that define high-value SaaS products. This is the fastest way to build a portfolio that actually sells.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a blinking cursor, mocking your lack of a breakthrough product. You’ve mastered the syntax, but you’re stuck in tutorial purgatory, paralyzed by the 'no project ideas' syndrome that kills more careers than bad code ever could. The secret isn't a flash of genius; it's mastering the engine that powers every successful SaaS: the pricing model.

By focusing your project on building robust pricing infrastructure, you pivot from 'coder' to 'product engineer.' This checklist transforms a mundane requirement into a high-leverage technical challenge, using modern tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to turn architectural theory into a production-ready feature.

steps.title: Define the Value Metric
steps.description: Identify what your users pay for—storage, API calls, or seat count. This step forces you to map user behavior to data structures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema for a SaaS platform that supports both flat-rate monthly subscriptions and usage-based billing metrics.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create-schema --file pricing_models.sql --description 'subscription and usage-based schema'

steps.title: Build the Tiered Access Logic
steps.description: Implement middleware that checks subscription status before allowing API execution. This is the backbone of SaaS security.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Python decorator for FastAPI that checks if a user's subscription tier has access to a specific protected endpoint.

steps.title: Simulate Stripe Integration
steps.description: Create a mock payment gateway service to handle webhooks and subscription lifecycle events without needing live API keys immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust mock Stripe webhook handler that processes 'invoice.payment_succeeded' and 'customer.subscription.deleted' events.

steps.title: Construct the Usage Meter
steps.description: Build an asynchronous consumer that tracks user activity and updates their usage quota in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design an async worker using Redis to track and increment user API usage counters, triggering a limit-exceeded flag when quotas are hit.

steps.title: Test Edge Case Resilience
steps.description: Ensure your system doesn't break when a payment fails or a user downgrades mid-cycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive test suite for the pricing engine, covering downgrades, credit card failures, and trial expirations.

pro_tips: Never hardcode pricing tiers; store them in a database or config service so you can A/B test prices without re-deploying.
Always implement a 'grace period' logic for payment failures to keep churn low and user trust high.
Use a 'usage buffer'—alert users at 80% usage rather than just cutting them off at 100% to improve UX.
Treat your pricing engine as a standalone microservice; it makes your portfolio project look infinitely more professional.
Build a 'Preview Mode' in your UI that dynamically shows how much a user would save by upgrading to the next tier.
content.next_steps: Deploy your pricing engine to a staging environment, integrate a real Stripe test-mode account, and document the architecture in a technical blog post to show off your logic.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to build any SaaS product with a sophisticated revenue engine. Don't let your code sit in a private repo—share your work, get feedback, and iterate until you have a production-ready application. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
